title: Employ Cryptographic Mechanisms to Protect the Confidentiality of Remote Access Sessions
description: employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of approved cryptographic mechanisms for remote access sessions is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Implemented
checks.purpose: Verify that RDP enforces NLA and TLS encryption, FIPS-compliant algorithms are enabled via Group Policy or registry, and weak SSL/TLS versions are disabled for remote access

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of approved cryptographic mechanisms for remote access sessions is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Implemented
checks.purpose: Verify that RDP enforces NLA and TLS encryption, FIPS-compliant algorithms are enabled via Group Policy, weak SSL/TLS versions are disabled, and IPSec is configured for server-to-server remote access

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of approved cryptographic mechanisms for remote access sessions is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Implemented
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH enforces strong ciphers, MACs, and key exchange algorithms, weak algorithms are explicitly disabled, and SSH protocol version 1 is not permitted

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of approved cryptographic mechanisms for remote access sessions is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Cryptographic Mechanisms for Remote Access Sessions are Implemented
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH enforces strong ciphers, MACs, and key exchange algorithms, weak algorithms are explicitly disabled, and SSH protocol version 1 is not permitted
